content: Self-Refine's iterative single-perspective refinement framework can be augmented by integrating multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to create a hybrid self-improvement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no extra training or supervision while cutting down on unnecessary iterations caused by blind spots in single-perspective feedback. This hybrid system would address a key unspoken limitation of vanilla Self-Refine: that a model can't catch its own blind spots if it only evaluates outputs from its original starting perspective.
